channel.title: The Grant Desk - Research & Journalism, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Research & Journalism, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Royal Academy of Fine Arts Antwerp: Call for Two-Year Research Proposals (2027 start)
item.description:
Organisation: Royal Academy of Fine Arts Antwerp (AP University of Applied Sciences and Arts)

Location: Antwerp, Belgium. Salaried research position; applicant must be legally employable in Belgium.

Award: Two-year research projects: 55% employment for the research assignment over 24 months (contractual project staff member) plus EUR 2,000 in operating funds (EUR 1,000 per project year). No application fee.

Duration: Two-year projects (24 months). Projects start at the earliest 20 September 2027.

Deadline: 03 Nov 2026

The Royal Academy of Fine Arts Antwerp (AP University of Applied Sciences and Arts) invites applications for two-year artistic research projects starting 20 September 2027. Two-year research projects provide 55% employment for the research assignment over 24 months (as a contractual project staff member) plus EUR 2,000 in operating funds (EUR 1,000 per project year). Proposals are developed in collaboration with the Academy's research groups and must align with, endorse, and further develop the mission and vision of the Royal Academy of Fine Arts Antwerp. PRECONDITIONS: the proposal must be in line with the Academy's mission/vision; the applicant must be legally employable by the Royal Academy of Fine Arts Antwerp/AP Hogeschool on a contractual basis; and, at the time of application, the applicant must hold a Master's degree in Visual Arts awarded by a Belgian higher education institution (a non-Belgian degree is possible only if validated or recognised through the NARIC procedure, with proof included or a declared willingness to undergo it). PROCEDURE (two steps): STEP 1, apply via the Expression of Interest form sent to research_royalacademyantwerp@ap.be before 3 November 2026, 12:00 noon (applicants informed no later than end November 2026 whether they may develop a full dossier); STEP 2, submit the full dossier via the two-year project application form before 1 February 2027, 12:00 noon. Evaluation by an independent reading committee aims to conclude in May 2027. Contact: research_royalacademyantwerp@ap.be.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Royal Conservatoire Antwerp: Call for Artistic Research Proposals (2027 start)
item.description:
Organisation: Royal Conservatoire Antwerp (AP University of the Arts)

Location: Antwerp, Belgium. Salaried research position; international applicants must hold a valid Belgian work permit.

Award: Salaried research position (contractual appointment). Start projects: 20% employment for 12 months + EUR 1,000 operating resources. One-year projects: 55% employment for 12 months + EUR 1,000. Two-year projects: 55% employment for 24 months + EUR 2,000. No application fee.

Duration: Projects start at the earliest 20 September 2027. Start and one-year projects run 12 months; two-year projects run 24 months.

Deadline: 21 Sep 2026

The Royal Conservatoire Antwerp (AP University of the Arts) invites applications for artistic research projects starting 20 September 2027, in three types: start projects, one-year projects, and two-year research projects. Applications are embedded in one or more of the Conservatoire's four research groups, supervised by a supervisor, and must align with the Conservatoire's mission and vision. FOCAL POINTS across disciplines: research into improvisation and intuitive creation and performance practices; new dramaturgical and scenographic approaches in cross-over projects; methodologies for interdisciplinary creation processes; sustainable relationships between artists and urban communities; the possibilities and limitations of inclusion in education and/or on stage; and well-being and resilience in music and performing arts. FUNDING (salaried research position): start projects, 20% employment for 12 months plus EUR 1,000 operating resources; one-year projects, 55% employment for 12 months plus EUR 1,000; two-year projects, 55% employment for 24 months plus EUR 2,000. PRECONDITIONS: the applicant holds a relevant master's degree and conducts research situated in or arising from their own artistic practice with the capacity for discursivity; the proposal must align with the Conservatoire's mission/vision; the applicant must be able to enter contractual employment with the Royal Conservatoire Antwerp/AP University of the Arts for the duration of the project (international applicants must hold a valid Belgian work permit); proposals may be submitted only in Dutch or English. PROCEDURE (two steps): STEP 1, submit an expression of interest by 21 September 2026, 12:00 noon (applicants informed by the week of 5 October whether they may develop a full proposal with a research group); STEP 2, submit the full project dossier by 1 December 2026, 12:00 noon. Evaluation by an independent reading committee aims to conclude in March 2027. Contact: Kevin Voets, Head of Research, kevin.voets@ap.be.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Paid Research Assistant: AI and the War in Ukraine
item.description:
Organisation: Bogna Konior (NYU Shanghai)

Location: Remote-friendly.

Award: USD 18 per hour; 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule.

Duration: Ongoing part-time research assistantship; start as soon as possible.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Bogna Konior, Assistant Professor of Media Theory at NYU Shanghai, is hiring a paid research assistant for a project on Ukraine and artificial intelligence. The work supports a forthcoming book with MIT Press, plus an independent scenario she is writing on the future of AI with the war as a lens. WHAT THE WORK LOOKS LIKE: going month-by-month through news archives (New York Times, Reuters, Financial Times, and specialist outlets), annotating and cataloguing coverage of AI, drones, and autonomous systems in the war, plus other developments she will direct you to; building and maintaining a structured Notion database covering chronology, companies, industries, and key actors; and organizing and editing her existing notes. WHO SHE IS LOOKING FOR: interest in and general knowledge of AI; prior research and annotation experience, careful and detail-oriented, no sloppy citations; comfortable with Notion and with AI tools; Ukrainian language skill a strong plus (not required); familiarity with AI, war, tech, and geopolitics news sources is great. Remote-friendly, start ASAP. She will also consider people with no prior experience, as well as current and previous students. TERMS: USD 18 per hour, 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule. TO APPLY: email bogna@nyu.edu with a short introduction and background (link to website, socials, CV, LinkedIn, or portfolio if you have one); where you are based and your weekly availability; and one recent AI-related story, company, or development you find interesting and why (a vibe check). Bonus: a sample of past research or writing if you have it.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: RYSE Innovation Hub: Youth Innovation Advisor
item.description:
Organisation: RYSE Innovation Hub

Location: 100% remote.

Award: USD 480 monthly compensation. No application fee.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027; up to 4 hours per week; 100% remote.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

RYSE Innovation Hub seeks Youth Innovation Advisors: young people ages 18 to 24 who are passionate about adolescent health and wellness. Youth Innovation Advisors support projects that strengthen youth engagement practices and help shape health solutions created with young people in mind. The role is 100% remote, running from September 2026 to July 2027, with a time commitment of up to 4 hours per week. WHO IT'S FOR: young people ages 18 to 24 passionate about adolescent health and wellness. COMPENSATION: USD 480 monthly. DEADLINE: 20 July 2026, 11:59 PM ET. TO APPLY: submit the application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL) Research Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL)

Location: Remote; international organisation. Experience working in or with global majority countries encouraged.

Award: USD 5,000 stipend for a 6-8 month fellowship. No application fee.

Duration: 6-8 month research fellowship; rolling applications

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL) Research Fellowship supports curious, passionate mid-career researchers and practitioners to explore frontier questions on digital public infrastructure (DPI) and data governance. Fellows bring their own knowledge and research agenda while contributing to DIAL's core thematic priorities: creating more value for more people from data; understanding how digital systems and services impact people, including and especially the most marginalised; and accelerating the adoption of data-sharing and governance practices that fuel innovation and empower people. SUPPORT: a USD 5,000 stipend for research completed over 6-8 months, plus mentorship from DIAL staff and industry experts, access to DIAL's network and resources, collaboration with partner organisations, and support amplifying the work. Each fellow produces two preliminary pieces and one final, more extensive output (research papers or reports, policy briefs, expert-comment series, etc.). ELIGIBILITY: professionals from academia, the private sector, and civil society, typically with 8-15 years of experience in digital development, policy, or research; demonstrated work at the intersection of technology and society (including DPI and data governance); a track record of high-quality writing, research, or policy briefs; experience working in or with global majority countries; and strong analytical and communication skills. TO APPLY (rolling basis): submit a CV or resume, a writing sample or portfolio, and a research proposal. Questions: fellows@dial.global. Apply via https://dial.global/apply-to-our-fellows-program/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Digital Democracy Centre x TrygFonden Fellowship Program 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Digital Democracy Centre (University of Southern Denmark), with support from TrygFonden

Location: Odense, Denmark (University of Southern Denmark). Flexible mix of in-office stays at the DDC in Odense and remote periods; stays planned to the Danish working calendar.

Award: Either a salary subsidy or a stipend to help with travel and/or housing; specific benefits (financial support, access to DDC research resources, flexible working spaces, speaker series, networks) vary by agreement between the fellow, DDC, and partnering organizations. Applicants describe the posts they seek support for (flights, housing, research costs, salary compensation, stipend, etc.).

Duration: Flexible; typically 2-6 months (minimum two months, can include shorter DDC stays and remote periods), longer in exceptional cases. Fellowships start anywhere in 2027.

Deadline: 01 Sep 2026

The Digital Democracy Centre (DDC) at the University of Southern Denmark, with support from TrygFonden, is calling for applications for its 2027 fellowship cohort. The program fosters collaboration between researchers, journalists, and policymakers exploring the impact of digital technology and AI on media, politics, and democracy. Fellows collaborate with a self-selected DDC mentor to generate new knowledge, contribute to public debate, develop networks, and produce tangible outreach outputs such as policy briefs, educational packages, and talks. THREE FELLOWSHIP TYPES: (1) academic fellows researching digital technologies and democracy; (2) journalist fellows covering digital technology and democracy; (3) policy fellows contributing to the regulatory debate. Target group: researchers, journalism graduates, and policymakers in research/educational institutions, media organizations, ministries, NGOs, etc. PRIORITIES: projects with clear relevance to Denmark or Danish society (including comparative/international projects yielding insights relevant to a Danish context); in 2027, projects related to DDC's Hybrid Warfare Initiative (societal resilience to new forms of international conflict, with a focus on digital technologies and AI) are also prioritized. EXPECTATIONS: applicants must secure confirmation of mentorship from their desired DDC mentor BEFORE applying (applications without a confirmed mentor are automatically rejected; PhD students and research assistants are generally not eligible as mentors, and Sten Schaumburg-Muller is unavailable as a mentor for the 2027 call); applicants must also coordinate support from their current supervisors/managers. Fellows engage in DDC activities, seminars/workshops, and public outreach, and submit a short report within two months of finishing. APPLICATION (in English): preliminary project title, fellowship type, brief project description (max 1000 chars), detailed description and timeline (max 2400 chars, including duration, confirmed mentor, and remote/in-office work plan), description of activities and collaboration with external practice partners (max 1000 chars), expected product/impact/output (max 500 chars), a CV (max 5 pages), and an overview of the posts for which financial support is sought. Submit the application form (PDF or Word) plus CV as PDFs to ddc@sdu.dk with subject line 'DDC / TrygFonden Fellowship application'. Deadline 1 September 2026; decisions communicated no later than 1 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Dedalus Foundation Senior Fellowship 2027
item.description:
Organisation: The Dedalus Foundation

Location: United States (remote, project-based)

Award: Stipend up to USD 30,000

Duration: Up to one year

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The Dedalus Foundation, established by artist Robert Motherwell, awards a Senior Fellowship supporting critical and historical studies related to painting, sculpture, and allied arts of the twentieth century. The fellowship is open to art historians, critics, curators, and other writers and scholars who have demonstrated their abilities through previous accomplishments. Awards provide a stipend of up to USD 30,000 for projects lasting up to one year, with the amount varying by project needs. Applicants must be U.S. citizens and may not be currently pursuing academic degrees. Applications are submitted online and reference letters through a separate form. A distinguished scholar jury selects recipients, with awards announced by mid-December.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Justice + Joy Summer Research Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Justice + Joy

Location: United States; fully virtual program. Capstone showcase tentatively in person (mid-September 2026), with travel and lodging covered.

Award: USD 2,000 stipend paid upon successful program completion, plus skills-based training, guest speaker sessions, and peer community. All travel and lodging for the in-person capstone are covered.

Duration: Seven-week virtual program, 9 July to 20 August 2026, with a capstone showcase mid-September 2026. Weekly Wednesday sessions 5:00-6:30 PM ET plus approximately 2-4 hours of independent work per week.

Deadline: Closed 09 Jul 2026

The Justice + Joy Research Fellowship is a seven-week virtual summer program for girls, young women, and gender-expansive young people of color (ages 18-26) in the United States who want to explore issues impacting their lives, communities, and futures. Fellows identify a topic they care about, such as mental health, education, reproductive justice, immigration, AI and technology, gender-based violence, beauty standards, young parenthood, or another issue affecting their communities, and develop an original research project grounded in their experiences, questions, and ideas. Throughout the summer, fellows participate in weekly virtual sessions, engage with guest speakers, build community with peers from across the country, and receive mentorship and support as they develop their research. The fellowship culminates in a capstone showcase where fellows present their work to the Justice + Joy community, including organizational leaders, partners, and supporters. No prior research experience is required; the program seeks young people who are curious, committed to learning, and interested in using research as a tool for understanding, advocacy, and change. COMMITMENT: weekly virtual sessions on Wednesdays 5:00-6:30 PM ET (9 July - 20 August 2026); approximately 2-4 hours of independent work per week; required orientation on Wednesday 9 July; development of an individual research proposal or conceptual framework; presentation at the capstone showcase (tentatively in person, mid-September 2026, with all travel and lodging covered). WHAT YOU GET: a USD 2,000 stipend paid upon successful completion; skills-based training in research methods, social justice frameworks, and community advocacy; guest speaker sessions; a peer community; and a real audience for the research (the Justice + Joy board and funders). Apply via the Google Form. Note: an explicit application deadline is not stated; the required orientation and program start is 9 July 2026, so apply before then.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Horizon Europe Cluster 2: Culture, Creativity and Inclusive Society (HORIZON-CL2-2026-01-HERITAGE) — incl. AI and Creative Content topic
item.description:
Organisation: European Research Executive Agency (REA) / European Commission, Horizon Europe

Location: European Union and associated countries. Consortium-based; typically requires partners from multiple EU/Horizon member states.

Award: Indicative total budget €85.5 million across 7 topics (2026 call). Individual project amounts vary by topic. Up to 100% funding rate for non-profit research organizations. No application fee.

Duration: Typically 3-4 years; varies by topic.

Deadline: 23 Sep 2026

Horizon Europe Cluster 2 funds research on culture, creativity, and inclusive society. The 2026 call (HORIZON-CL2-2026-01-HERITAGE, launched May 12, 2026) covers 7 topics with an indicative budget of €85.5 million. Topic HERITAGE-04 is explicitly titled 'Towards a fair and transparent market for cultural and creative content in the era of generative AI,' covering: impacts of generative AI on creative industries employment; upskilling needs for creative workers; policy and regulatory frameworks; fair remuneration and transparency. Highly relevant for research into AI and cultural/creative industries, artists' rights in the AI era, and sociotechnical studies of AI and culture. ELIGIBILITY: consortium-based applications (minimum 3 organisations from 3 different EU/associated countries are typical for Horizon RIA grants; check call conditions on the Funding & Tenders portal for specific requirements). Deadline: 23 September 2026. Apply via the EU Funding & Tenders portal (ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Omidyar Network: Tech Journalism Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Omidyar Network

Location: Open to journalists and media organizations worldwide.

Award: $5,000-$25,000 per project. Rolling deadline through December 31, 2026. No application fee.

Duration: Per-project; scope and timeline defined in the proposal.

Deadline: 31 Dec 2026

The Omidyar Network Tech Journalism Fund offers rolling grants of $5,000-$25,000 for technology journalism projects. Relevant for journalists and media organizations investigating AI, tech policy, and AI's impacts on culture, society, and creative industries. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis through December 31, 2026 — one of the few currently open opportunities with a confirmed near-term deadline. Apply via the application form on omidyar.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: KB Researcher-in-Residence (RiR) 2027
item.description:
Organisation: KB, National Library of the Netherlands

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (two days per week on-site at the KB premises)

Award: Secondment at 0.5 FTE for 6 months based on your current salary, up to EUR 5,590 gross per month for a full-time-equivalent position, plus travel expenses, office facilities, and support from a research software engineer, research advisor, and collection and data specialists. No application fee.

Duration: 6 months at 0.5 FTE (two days per week on-site); two positions in 2027, starting Q1 and Q3

Deadline: 27 Sep 2026

ELIGIBILITY: two Researcher-in-Residence positions in 2027. Open to early-career researchers of any discipline (data science, social science, computer science, humanities) who are currently working on a PhD or within 5 years of their PhD end date. You must have, or be able to secure, an affiliation with a Dutch university or research institute willing to second you; external PhD students may use the funding to obtain such a contract. Your project must make use of one or more of the KB digital collections and you must be able to work with primarily Dutch data. You must be available at 0.5 FTE over 6 months and able to spend two days a week on-site at the KB in The Hague. COLLECTIONS: over 130 million digitized pages of newspapers, magazines, radio bulletins and books from 1470 onward (via Delpher and DBNL), a born-digital web collection of more than 23,000 websites, ground-truth and derived datasets on the KB Lab, and metadata including Linked Data. APPLY: use the official proposal template and submit a single PDF by 27 September 2026 to dh@kb.nl, after reading the call's terms and conditions; questions to dh@kb.nl. TIMELINE: deadline 27 September 2026; internal eligibility review followed by an external committee review; outcome notified November 2026; residencies start Q1 and Q3 2027. DISCIPLINES: digital humanities / data science / computational research with cultural-heritage collections. NOTE: the output is usually a dataset or tool prototype hosted on the KB Lab; you agree to share results via blog posts on the KB Lab and to publish resulting tools or data under an open license within 6 months of the residency. Proposals should align with the KB Research Agenda or the Cultural AI Lab.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: European Commission: Safeguarding Europe's Born-Digital Heritage (PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL)
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission (Pilot Projects and Preparatory Actions, Culture strand)

Location: European Union. Consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU member states required.

Award: Up to €1,985,000, covering up to 85% of eligible project costs (15% co-financing required). No application fee.

Duration: Project duration to be defined in the proposal; covers a scoping and mapping phase across EU member states.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

The European Commission is offering a single grant of up to €1,985,000 (85% co-financing) for a project focused on safeguarding born-digital cultural heritage across EU member states. Scope includes: assessing the preservation state of born-digital art, web archives, video games, and digital content created outside cultural institutions; mapping EU and national legal frameworks; identifying non-legislative barriers (technical, financial, organisational, cultural); and providing guidance on creating preservation copies of licensed or legally accessible materials. This is a scoping and advocacy project, not a digitisation grant. ELIGIBILITY: the application must be led by a consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU countries, which rules out solo applicants and single-country organisations. Grant reference: PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL. Deadline: 16 July 2026. Verify the current deadline at ec.europa.eu before applying (one secondary source listed 1 June 2026; the primary call-fiche PDF and the Cyprus government funding portal both confirm 16 July 2026).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEH: Collections Stewardship (December 2026 cycle)
item.description:
Organisation: National Endowment for the Humanities (NEH)

Location: United States. Applicants must be US-based institutions.

Award: Up to $350,000 for single organizations; up to $500,000 for consortia. Projects up to 3 years. No application fee.

Duration: Up to 3 years.

Deadline: 15 Dec 2026

NEH Collections Stewardship funds preservation projects at libraries, archives, museums, colleges/universities, and preservation-services organisations. Eligible activities include digitisation and reformatting of collections for public access through digital repositories, preservation planning, and environmental assessments. Relevant for organisations working with born-digital or digitally reformatted collections including software, AI artefacts, and digital cultural heritage. Applications for the December 2026 cycle open 31 August 2026; deadline 15 December 2026. A May 2026 cycle has already passed. ELIGIBILITY: institutional applicants only (libraries, archives, museums, colleges, universities, preservation-services organisations based in the US); individual researchers and for-profit entities are not eligible. Apply via grants.gov.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rodger and Joann McLennan Canadian War Art Research Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: National Gallery of Canada

Location: Ottawa, Canada; tenable only at the National Gallery of Canada.

Award: Up to $5,000 (including expenses and/or stipend). No application fee.

Duration: One calendar year, beginning 1 September 2026.

Deadline: Closed 26 Jun 2026

The National Gallery of Canada's Rodger and Joann McLennan Canadian War Art Research Fellowship encourages and supports advanced research in Canadian and Indigenous Canadian war and military art. Research must relate to any aspect of Canadian war or military art (including painting, drawing, sculpture, photography, printmaking, and other media) and must emphasise the use and investigation of the collections of the National Gallery of Canada, the Canadian War Museum, and Library and Archives Canada. The Fellowship supports original research by scholars undertaking publishing, exhibition, thesis, and dissertation work or their equivalents. AMOUNT: up to $5,000, including expenses and/or stipend. TENURE: one calendar year beginning 1 September 2026; tenable only at the National Gallery of Canada. ELIGIBILITY: open to art historians, curators, critics, conservators, graduate students, and independent or other professionals working in the visual arts, museology, and related disciplines in the humanities and social sciences; international applicants welcome. Researchers from outside Canada must ensure all visa requirements and documentation are in order before entry and the start of the Fellowship. Applications are invited from all qualified candidates, including Indigenous peoples, women, people of any sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression, racialized people, and people with disabilities. TO APPLY: submit a complete application in English or French as a single PDF by email to Amy Rose, Senior Manager, Library, Archives and Research Fellowship Program, at erefel@gallery.ca. Deadline: 26 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Civic Science Postdoctoral Fellow: Science Policy Collaborative (George Mason University) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: George Mason University (with UC Riverside), via the Science Policy Collaborative and Civic Science Fellows network

Location: Fairfax, Virginia, USA (hybrid eligible).

Award: Salary approximately USD 80,000 per year plus benefits. No application fee.

Duration: 18-month postdoctoral appointment (two contracts: 12 months + 6 months)

Deadline: Closed 05 Jul 2026

A grant-funded, full-time Civic Science Postdoctoral Fellowship supporting the Science Policy Collaborative, a national network that strengthens the development, sustainability and evaluation of U.S. science policy programs. The fellow helps shape an emerging field at the heart of a growing community of practice connecting science policy programs across the country. WHAT YOU'LL DO: conduct a national needs assessment of U.S. science policy programs and publish the findings; support the Collaborative's working groups by scoping deliverables and building partnerships to execute them; develop infrastructure for knowledge exchange (tools like logic models and evaluation frameworks); connect programs with national and international experts on the use of research evidence (URE) and evidence-informed policymaking; present at partner meetings and convenings; and receive mentorship from George Mason University, UC Riverside and other leaders of the Collaborative. WHO THEY'RE LOOKING FOR: someone with a doctoral degree, relevant experience, knowledge of science-for-policy programs, and skills in network coordination, needs assessment or program evaluation, and writing for broad audiences (see the posting for exact criteria). Fairfax, VA (hybrid eligible); full-time 18-month postdoctoral appointment issued as two contracts (12 months plus 6 months); salary about USD 80,000/year plus benefits. APPLY with a cover letter, CV, three references and a writing sample written for broad audiences; for full consideration apply by 5 July 2026 (open until filled). Questions to search coordinator Natalie V Lapidot-Croitoru, nlapidot@gmu.edu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
